name: Saag Vali Khichri
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over, wash, and drain the mung beans.
2. Wash and drain the rice.
3. Wash the spinach well and separate the leaves.
4. Peel the onion, cut it in half lengthwise, and slice into fine half-rounds.
5. Put the mung beans in a heavy 4-quart pot.
6. Add water and bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
7. Cover, lower heat, and simmer for two minutes.
8. Turn off the heat and let the pot sit, covered, for an hour.
9. Bring the water to a boil again.
10. Add the rice and ginger; bring to a simmer.
11. Cover, turn heat to low, and cook gently for one hour, stirring occasionally.
12. Stir in the spinach and salt; bring to a simmer.
13. Cover and cook gently for 30 minutes, stirring periodically to avoid sticking.
14. If the mixture seems too thick, add a little hot water.
15. Heat ghee in a skillet over medium heat.
16. When hot, add hing; stir immediately.
17. Add whole cumin seeds; stir immediately after adding.
18. Add onion and stir for two seconds.
19. Add ground cumin, coriander, and cayenne; stir for one minute.
20. Empty the contents of the skillet into the rice and bean pot.
21. Stir to combine.
22. Cover and cook for five more minutes.
23. Discard the ginger.
24. Sprinkle with black pepper.
25. Serve in individual bowls with some or all of the garnishes (lemon juice, butter, cilantro).
